questions.question: What is Minification & Bundle Optimization and why is it important?
questions.answer: Minification & Bundle Optimization is a fundamental concept that Minify CSS/JS, tree shaking, and reducing bundle sizes. It's important because it forms the foundation for building robust and efficient applications. Understanding Minification & Bundle Optimization allows developers to make better architectural decisions and write cleaner code.

questions.question: How do you apply Minification & Bundle Optimization in a real project?
questions.answer: In practice, Minification & Bundle Optimization is applied by following established patterns and best practices. Start with the simplest implementation, test thoroughly, and iterate. The key is to understand the underlying principles so you can adapt to different scenarios.

questions.question: What are common mistakes when working with Minification & Bundle Optimization?
questions.answer: Common mistakes include: not understanding the fundamentals before applying advanced patterns, ignoring edge cases, not testing adequately, and over-engineering solutions. The best approach is to start simple and add complexity only when needed.

questions.answer: Minification & Bundle Optimization is essentially about minify css/js, tree shaking, and reducing bundle sizes. Think of it as a building block — once you understand this concept, you can combine it with other concepts to build complex applications.
